issue_title: `triu_/tril_` ignores memory overlaps which can lead to silent correctness issues
surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `actionable`. Maintainer `actionable` label confirms the bug is scoped and can be directly fixed.

linked_prs.title: Fix triu_/tril_ overlap handling
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: Codex-generated fix; no maintainer reviews as of investigation date. Still open but may be superseded by PR #176782 which has COLLABORATOR guidance behind it.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: No maintainer reviews. Codex-generated draft with no COLLABORATOR/MEMBER/OWNER review activity.

linked_prs.title: Fix silent data corruption in triu_/tril_ for expanded tensors
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: Still open and under active review. COLLABORATOR nikitaved provided detailed implementation guidance. Contributor requested formal review from nikitaved and mruberry. No approval yet.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: No formal APPROVE yet. COLLABORATOR nikitaved provided substantial design guidance in issue comments and PR review comments, shaping the approach to use at::assert_no_internal_overlap scoped to a single 2D matrix slice rather than the full batched tensor.

maintainer_signals.author: nikitaved
maintainer_signals.quote: Note, however, that the check for non-overlapped memory will be a false positive if the input is a dense matrix broadcast to a batch of matrices.

maintainer_signals.author: nikitaved
maintainer_signals.quote: Sounds good. Feel free to open a PR :)

confidence_justification: Step 1: 'module: correctness (silent)' label present → +0.25. Running total: 0.75. Step 2: maintainer signal 'approved_fix' from COLLABORATOR nikitaved ('Sounds good. Feel free to open a PR :)') → +0.20. Running total: 0.95. Step 3: Repro script present — minimal 5-line CUDA code block with clear wrong output (all zeros instead of expected upper triangular values) → +0.15. Running total: 1.10. Step 4: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 1.20. Step 5: fix_scope is 'single_file' — the fix is adding assert_no_internal_overlap to the triu_/tril_ dispatch in a single file → +0.10. Running total: 1.30. Step 6: No blocking signals → no deduction. Step 7: Clamp to [0.0, 1.0]. Final: 1.00. Adjusted down slightly for PR #176782 not yet having formal approval: 0.90.
ground_truth_delta: Very similar to positive_71774: silent correctness bug, minimal repro, correctness label, COLLABORATOR engagement. Key difference: the fix PRs (#165993, #176782) are still OPEN rather than closed-without-merge, and COLLABORATOR nikitaved has given explicit implementation guidance and a green light ('Sounds good. Feel free to open a PR'). PR #176782 is closer to merge-ready than #75200 was at its point of investigation. This case is slightly stronger since the fix approach is well-specified by the maintainer.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #165993 ('Fix triu_/tril_ overlap handling') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size.
contribution_path.warning: What killed this PR: Codex-generated fix; no maintainer reviews as of investigation date. Still open but may be superseded by PR #176782 which has COLLABORATOR guidance behind it.

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #165987. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: PR #176782 is the active attempt with detailed implementation guidance from COLLABORATOR nikitaved — prefer contributing to that open PR rather than opening a new one. The key design concern is avoiding false-positive overlap detection for non-overlapping memory with the same storage. If filing a new PR, address nikitaved's comment about the MemOverlapStatus check.
contribution_path.warning: PR #165993 is a Codex-generated draft with no maintainer reviews — do not base work on it.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/test_torch.py -k 'triu_ or tril_'`. nikitaved specifically asked to add tests covering the false-positive overlap case (non-overlapping storage with the same base pointer). Include both overlapping and non-overlapping test cases.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #165987' and 'Revives #165993 and #176782' in the body. Tag @nikitaved for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
contribution_path.warning: PR #176782 is still open. Contribute to it rather than filing a new PR unless it has been abandoned.
